pages.title: The Monument
pages.text: It was here before the roads. Before the ruined villages and the collapsed towers and the names carved into stones you cannot read.$(br2)You did not build it. You cannot move it. It stands at the center of the world and the ground has settled around it like an old wound - not healed. Closed.$(br2)Something about it is still running.

pages.title: The Footprint
pages.text: The monument claims a tract of ground. Thirty-five blocks in every direction from its center. Within that boundary, the air is different. Quieter. As though the place is paying attention.$(br2)Whatever mechanism drove this structure was not broken when the civilization that built it ended. It was not sabotaged. It was not abandoned in grief or panic.$(br2)It was simply left to wait. It has been waiting a long time.
